hero.title: ToldYa!
hero.subtitle: Told you so! Prove it.
hero.description: Invite-only social predictions — post your call before it happens, vote in the Arena, build a reputation score, and prove you called it. Free on iOS and Android; no ads, no in-app purchases.
hero.structuredDataDescription: ToldYa! — invite-only social network for predictions, Arena voting, comments, and reputation. Android com.zuki.apps.toldya; iOS App Store id6756342206 by Zuki Apps.
hero.metaDescription: Make predictions with friends, track results & streaks. Free on iOS & Android.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy explains how Zuki Apps (“we”, “us”) processes personal data when you use the ToldYa! mobile application and related pages at zukiapps.com/toldya (the “Service”). ToldYa! is an invite-only social network for user-generated predictions, Arena voting, mandatory author resolution, outcome disputes, optional private rooms with friends, comments, direct messages, and reputation scoring.

section1.title: 1. Who we are
section1.content: The data controller for ToldYa! is Zuki Apps. Contact details are at the end of this Policy.
section1.content2: This Policy applies to the ToldYa! app (iOS and Android) and the legal pages we publish for the product. It does not cover third-party websites or app stores, which have their own policies.

section2.title: 2. Information we collect
section2.content: We collect information you provide and information generated when you use the Service:
items: Account data: email address, username, display name, password (stored in hashed form by our authentication provider), optional profile bio and avatar image, language preference, and invitation-related metadata.

Social & content data: predictions (text, category, target dates, status, visibility), votes, likes, comments, retweets/shares, follower relationships, reputation or category scores, and direct messages you send or receive.

Resolution & dispute data: outcomes submitted by prediction authors (including optional notes and evidence links), resolution timestamps, dispute reports filed by other members, and moderation-queue records related to outcomes.
Private room data: room names, membership lists, invitations, and predictions scoped to a room (visible only to room members, excluded from public feeds and global leaderboards).
Safety & moderation data: content reports, block lists, automated content-filter signals, and enforcement actions taken on your account or content.
Media: photos or videos you attach to predictions, if you choose to upload them.

Device & usage data: device type, operating system, app version, push notification tokens (if you enable notifications), IP-derived region (where available), crash logs, and analytics events via Google Firebase Analytics and Crashlytics.
Support & safety: information you send when contacting support or reporting content or users.
Communications metadata: timestamps and delivery status for notifications and messages.

section3.title: 3. How we use your information
section3.content: We use personal data to provide and secure the social network, including:
items: Create and manage your account, invitation access, and profile
Display feeds, Arena views, voting results, comments, and notifications
Operate mandatory prediction resolution, overdue reminders, and dispute review
Operate private rooms, membership, and room-scoped feeds
Operate follower features, search, and reputation scoring (excluding room-only predictions from global stats)
Send push or in-app notifications you have enabled (including resolution reminders)
Detect abuse, enforce our Terms, and respond to reports and blocks
Maintain reliability, debug crashes, and improve the Service
Comply with law and respond to lawful requests
section3.content2: We do not sell your personal information. We do not use your data for third-party advertising profiles in the current version of the app.

section4.title: 4. Legal bases (where applicable)
section4.content: Depending on your location, we rely on: performance of a contract (providing the Service you request); legitimate interests (security, fraud prevention, product improvement, and community safety, balanced against your rights); consent (e.g. optional notifications or permissions you grant on device); and legal obligation (records we must keep).

section5.title: 5. How we share information
section5.content: Other members can see your public profile (username, display name, avatar, bio, scores, and public predictions and interactions) according to product design. Predictions marked as private-room are visible only to members of that room and are not shown in public home or following feeds or global leaderboards. We do not display your email address to other users.
section5.content2: When you use the in-app “Share result” feature, an image or link is generated on your device and sent through your chosen third-party app (for example Messages, WhatsApp, or social networks). We do not receive that exported image unless you separately send it to us. Those third-party services have their own privacy policies. We use service providers that process data on our behalf, including Google Firebase (authentication, cloud database, file storage, analytics, crash reporting, and hosting as configured in our project). These providers act under contractual terms appropriate to their role. We may disclose information if required by law, to protect users and the public, or in connection with a merger or asset sale, with notice where required.

section6.title: 6. User-generated content & moderation
section6.content: Predictions, comments, messages, room content, and media are user-generated. We provide in-app tools to report content or users and to block other users, as required by Apple App Store and Google Play policies for apps with user-generated content. Reports and blocks are reviewed by our team; safety-related reports are prioritized. We also use automated content filtering on create flows where configured.
section6.content2: We may review, remove, restrict, or correct content and accounts that violate our Terms or applicable law, including after an outcome dispute is filed. Removed content may remain in backups for a limited period before deletion. For child safety, see our Child Safety Standards page.

section7.title: 7. Retention
section7.content: We keep personal data while your account is active and as needed to operate the Service. After account deletion, we delete or anonymize profile and content data within a reasonable period (typically within 30 days), except where we must retain limited records for legal, security, or dispute purposes.

section8.title: 8. Account deletion
section8.content: You can delete your account in the app (Settings → Delete account) or by following the instructions on our Delete Account page. Deletion is permanent and removes your profile, predictions, votes, comments, and messages from the live Service, subject to backup and legal retention described above.

section9.title: 9. Your rights
section9.content: Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have rights to access, correct, delete, restrict, or object to certain processing, and to data portability. You may withdraw consent where processing is consent-based. To exercise rights, contact us using the details below; we may need to verify your identity.
section9.content2: Users in the EU/EEA, UK, and Israel may also lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority. California residents may have additional rights under applicable state law; contact us to submit a request.

section10.title: 10. Security
section10.content: We implement technical and organizational measures appropriate to a consumer social application (access controls, encrypted transport, and provider security features). No system is completely secure; use a strong password and protect your device.

section11.title: 11. International transfers
section11.content: Data may be processed in countries where we or our providers operate (including the United States and regions used by Google Cloud/Firebase). Where required, we rely on appropriate safeguards such as standard contractual clauses or equivalent mechanisms.

section12.title: 12. Children
section12.content: ToldYa! is not directed to children under 13 (or the minimum age required in your country). We do not knowingly collect personal data from children. If you believe a child has provided data, contact us and we will delete it.

section13.title: 13. Changes to this Policy
section13.content: We may update this Policy from time to time. We will post the revised version with a new “Last updated” date. Material changes may be notified in-app or by email where appropriate. Continued use after the effective date constitutes acceptance.

section14.title: 14. Prediction resolution & disputes
section14.content: After a prediction’s voting deadline passes, the author must submit the outcome (fulfilled, failed, or void/cancelled) with any required note. Other members may dispute a submitted outcome if they believe it is incorrect. Disputes are stored with your account identifier, the prediction reference, optional reason text, and timestamp, and are routed to our moderation queue for human review.
section14.content2: Submitting a dispute does not instantly change the displayed outcome. We aim to review non–child-safety disputes within a reasonable period (typically within 14 business days). We may correct an outcome, request additional information from the author, or decline a dispute if insufficient evidence is provided. Overdue unresolved public predictions may trigger reminders and, in some cases, limit your ability to post new public predictions until resolved.

section15.title: 15. Private rooms
section15.content: Private rooms let invited members make predictions visible only within that room. We process room names, creator and member user IDs, invitation activity, and room-scoped predictions, votes, and comments. This data is not included in public home or following feeds or global reputation leaderboards.
section15.content2: Room membership is visible to other members of the same room. The same community standards, reporting, blocking, and moderation processes apply inside private rooms. Room data is deleted or anonymized when you delete your account or when a room is removed, subject to backup and legal retention described above.

section16.title: 16. Sharing outside the Service
section16.content: The Service may let you generate a shareable image card (for example after a resolved prediction) containing your username, prediction text, outcome, vote summary, and branding. That export is created on your device and shared through third-party apps you choose (via the system share sheet). We do not automatically post shared cards on your behalf.
section16.content2: Once you share content outside ToldYa!, the receiving platform’s terms and privacy policy govern that copy. You are responsible for what you share and with whom. Deep links included in shares may require sign-in or an invitation to view the full Arena.

sectionContact.title: Contact
sectionContact.content: Questions about this Privacy Policy or your data:

privacy.metaDescription: Privacy Policy for ToldYa! — account, UGC, resolution disputes, private rooms, Firebase, moderation, your rights, and account deletion.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.intro: These Terms of Use (“Terms”) govern your access to ToldYa! (“Service”, “app”), an invite-only social network operated by Zuki Apps. By creating an account or using the Service, you agree to these Terms and our Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, do not use the Service.

section1.title: 1. Acceptance
section1.content: You must be legally able to enter a binding agreement in your jurisdiction. If you use the Service on behalf of an organization, you represent that you have authority to bind that organization.
section1.content2: We may update these Terms. We will post the updated version with a new “Last updated” date. Material changes may be communicated in-app. Continued use after changes take effect constitutes acceptance.

section2.title: 2. The Service
section2.content: ToldYa! is a social network where members post time-bound predictions, vote on others’ predictions in the Arena, comment, follow users, send direct messages, resolve outcomes after deadlines, optionally dispute outcomes, join private rooms with invited friends, share result cards, and build reputation scores by category. The Service is for entertainment, opinion, and community discussion only.
section2.content2: There is no real-money wagering, betting, prizes with monetary value, or virtual currency redeemable for money. Private rooms are for friendly predictions among invited members only—not gambling or financial contracts. Predictions are not financial, legal, or professional advice.

section3.title: 3. Accounts & invitations
section3.content: Access currently requires a valid invitation code. You must provide accurate registration information and keep your credentials confidential. You are responsible for all activity under your account.
section3.content2: You may not share, sell, or publicly distribute invitation codes in a way that circumvents our access controls. We may refuse registration, reclaim usernames, or close accounts at our discretion.

section4.title: 4. Your responsibilities
section4.content: As a member of a community social network, you agree to:
items: Treat other users with respect and comply with applicable laws
Post only content you have the right to share and that is appropriate for a general-audience social app
Use your real identity only as permitted by the product (no impersonation or deceptive profiles)
Not scrape, reverse engineer, or overload the Service without permission
Not manipulate votes, rankings, or reputation through bots, coordinated inauthentic behavior, or exploits
Resolve your own predictions honestly after the deadline when you are the author
Not submit false or misleading resolution outcomes
Report content or users you believe violate these Terms

section5.title: 5. Community standards — prohibited content
section5.content: You may not post, upload, transmit, or link to content that includes or promotes:
items: Nudity, pornography, sexually explicit material, or solicitation of sexual services
Sexual content involving minors or any exploitation of minors (zero tolerance; we report to authorities where required)
Graphic violence, gore, or content primarily intended to shock or glorify violence
Threats, harassment, bullying, stalking, or hate speech targeting individuals or groups
Illegal activity, fraud, or instructions to cause harm
Self-harm or suicide promotion (if you are in crisis, seek local emergency or crisis services)
Spam, malware, phishing, or deceptive schemes
Personal data of others without their consent (doxxing)
Intellectual property you do not have rights to use
section5.content2: We may remove content and suspend or terminate accounts without prior notice for violations.

section6.title: 6. Additional prohibited conduct
items: Use the Service if you are barred under applicable law
Circumvent security, invitation, or rate limits
Create multiple accounts to evade enforcement
Use automated means to access the Service except through features we expressly provide
Collect other users’ data for marketing or harassment

section7.title: 7. Your content & license
section7.content: You retain ownership of content you submit. You grant Zuki Apps a worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free license to host, store, reproduce, display, distribute, and adapt your content as needed to operate, promote, and improve the Service (including showing it to other users and in feeds).
section7.content2: You represent that your content does not violate these Terms or third-party rights. You may delete your predictions where the product allows; deletion of your account removes associated content as described in our Privacy Policy.

section8.title: 8. Moderation & enforcement
section8.content: We may (but are not obligated to) monitor, filter, or review content. We may remove content, limit features, suspend, or permanently terminate accounts. Enforcement decisions are at our sole discretion to protect the community and comply with law.
section8.content2: If you believe content or an account violates these Terms, use in-app reporting where available or contact support. We aim to review user reports and block actions within 24 hours for standard community issues, and sooner for child-safety or imminent-harm reports. Outcome disputes are reviewed separately and do not guarantee an immediate change to the displayed result.

section9.title: 9. Voting, resolution & predictions disclaimer
section9.content: Votes and comments reflect community opinion, not facts verified by us. After the deadline, the prediction author must submit the outcome. We do not guarantee accuracy of predictions, votes, or author-submitted outcomes. Members may dispute an outcome through the in-app process; disputes are reviewed by us but do not instantly overturn the author’s submission.
section9.content2: You use others’ content and community-reported outcomes at your own risk. Reputation scores and leaderboards exclude unresolved predictions and room-only content as described in the product.

section10.title: 10. Privacy
section10.content: Our Privacy Policy explains how we collect and use personal data, including account deletion. It is incorporated into these Terms by reference.

section11.title: 11. Termination & account deletion
section11.content: You may stop using the Service at any time and delete your account in Settings or via our Delete Account page. We may suspend or terminate your access for any violation of these Terms or for risk to users or the Service.
section11.content2: Upon termination, licenses you granted survive only as needed for legal compliance and backup deletion cycles.

section12.title: 12. Disclaimers & limitation of liability
section12.content: THE SERVICE IS PROVIDED “AS IS” AND “AS AVAILABLE” WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW. WE DISCLAIM IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT.
section12.content2: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, ZUKI APPS WILL NOT BE LIABLE FOR INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, OR LOSS OF PROFITS, DATA, OR GOODWILL, ARISING FROM YOUR USE OF THE SERVICE. OUR TOTAL LIABILITY FOR ANY CLAIM RELATING TO THE SERVICE IS LIMITED TO THE GREATER OF USD $50 OR THE AMOUNT YOU PAID US IN THE TWELVE MONTHS BEFORE THE CLAIM (TYPICALLY ZERO FOR THE FREE APP).

section13.title: 13. Governing law
section13.content: These Terms are governed by the laws of the State of Israel, without regard to conflict-of-law rules, except where mandatory consumer protections in your country require otherwise. Courts in Israel shall have exclusive jurisdiction where permitted, subject to mandatory local rights.

section14.title: 14. Author resolution obligations
section14.content: If you create a prediction, you are responsible for submitting the correct outcome after the voting deadline passes (fulfilled, failed, or void/cancelled). You must not deliberately misstate results. Overdue predictions may display an “awaiting resolution” state, trigger reminders, and may restrict your ability to create new public predictions until resolved.
section14.content2: Optional notes or evidence links you provide at resolution are stored with the prediction and may be visible to members who can view that prediction. We may void or correct outcomes that violate these Terms or after a upheld dispute.

section15.title: 15. Outcome disputes
section15.content: Members who are not the author may dispute a submitted outcome if they believe it is incorrect. Disputes must be submitted in good faith through the in-app flow. Filing a dispute does not immediately change the outcome, vote history, or reputation score.
section15.content2: We review disputes through our moderation process and may uphold, decline, or correct an outcome. Repeated frivolous or abusive disputes may result in account restrictions. Our decision on disputes is made in our reasonable discretion to protect community integrity.

section16.title: 16. Private rooms
section16.content: Private rooms allow invited members to post predictions visible only within that room. Room creators and members must comply with these Terms and community standards. Room predictions are not wagers, bets, or contracts for money or prizes.
section16.content2: Room membership is limited as shown in the app. Do not use private rooms to harass others, share illegal content, or circumvent enforcement. We may remove rooms, predictions, or members that violate these Terms. Global leaderboards and public reputation exclude room-only predictions.

section17.title: 17. Sharing result cards
section17.content: The Service may let you export an image card summarizing a resolved prediction (including your username, question, outcome, and branding) and share it through third-party apps. You are responsible for ensuring your shares comply with these Terms and the policies of the platform where you post.
section17.content2: Do not use shared cards to misrepresent outcomes, impersonate others, or promote prohibited content. Shared deep links may require an account or invitation to view the underlying Arena.

section18.title: 18. Safety tools (Apple & Google requirements)
section18.content: Because ToldYa! includes user-generated content, we provide:
items: In-app reporting on predictions, comments, profiles, and messages (where available)
The ability to block users so their content is hidden from your feed
Published community standards (these Terms) and a Child Safety Standards page
A published contact for support and a designated child-safety contact
Moderation and enforcement, including removal of violating content and account suspension or termination
section18.content2: You must use these tools responsibly. False or malicious reports may lead to enforcement action against your account.

deleteAccount.title: Delete your ToldYa! account
deleteAccount.intro: You can permanently delete your ToldYa! account and associated personal data. This action cannot be undone.

warning.title: Important
warning.content: Deleting your account removes your profile, predictions, votes, comments, direct messages, follower relationships, private room memberships, dispute reports, and reputation data from the live Service. Some information may remain in encrypted backups for a limited time before automatic deletion, or where we must retain records by law.

steps.title: Delete in the app (recommended)
steps.step3: Choose Delete account (or Account → Delete account)
steps.step4: Read the confirmation carefully and confirm deletion
steps.step5: You will be signed out and your account will be scheduled for removal

stepsEmail.title: Cannot access the app?
stepsEmail.content: Email zuki.apps.dev@gmail.com from the address linked to your account with the subject “Delete my ToldYa account”. Include your username so we can verify ownership. We may ask for additional verification before processing the request.

data.title: Data removed
data.intro: After successful deletion, we delete or anonymize, among other things:
data.item1: Account credentials and profile (email, username, display name, bio, avatar)
data.item2: Predictions, votes, comments, likes, and retweets
data.item3: Direct messages and notification history tied to your account
data.item4: Follower / following relationships, private room memberships, and category scores
data.item5: Resolution notes, dispute reports tied to your account, and push notification tokens for your devices

retention.title: Retention
retention.content: We aim to complete deletion from primary systems within 30 days. Limited logs or backups may persist briefly for security and disaster recovery, then expire according to our retention schedule.

contact.title: Questions
contact.content: For help with deletion or privacy requests:

childSafety.title: Child Safety Standards
childSafety.intro: ToldYa! is an invite-only social network operated by Zuki Apps. We maintain zero tolerance for child sexual abuse and exploitation (CSAE). This page describes our published standards, in-app reporting, moderation practices, and how to contact us regarding child safety.

section1.title: 1. Zero tolerance
section1.content: We prohibit any content or conduct that sexually exploits, endangers, or otherwise harms children. Accounts involved in CSAE may be removed immediately and permanently. Where required by law, we report to regional and national authorities and cooperate with law enforcement.

section2.title: 2. Standards against CSAE
section2.content: The following are prohibited on ToldYa! and may result in account termination and referral to authorities:
items: Child sexual abuse material (CSAM) in any form, including real, synthetic, or illustrated sexual content depicting minors
Sexualization of minors, grooming, or solicitation of minors
Content that facilitates exploitation or trafficking of children
Accounts that misrepresent age to contact or exploit minors
Links or instructions to obtain CSAM or to harm children
section2.content2: These rules are also reflected in our Terms of Use. We use a combination of automated signals, user reports, and human review to enforce them.

section3.title: 3. Age requirement
section3.content: ToldYa! is not directed to children under 13 (or the minimum age required in your country). Registration requires an invitation code and is intended for adults and teens who meet the minimum age. We may suspend accounts we believe belong to underage users.

section4.title: 4. In-app reporting
section4.content: Users can report child safety concerns in the app. Reporting options are available on predictions, comments, profiles, and messages (where the product provides a report or flag control). Reports are routed to our moderation queue and prioritized when they involve potential harm to minors.
section4.content2: When reporting, choose the category that best describes the issue and add any helpful context. You may also block users to prevent further contact.

section5.title: 5. Moderation & response
section5.content: We review child-safety reports as a priority. Confirmed CSAM is removed, preserving only what is legally required for evidence or mandatory reporting. Offending accounts are banned. We may preserve logs and content hashes as needed for compliance and cooperation with authorities.
section5.content2: We continually improve detection, reporting flows, and reviewer guidance. No system is perfect; we rely on our community and staff to identify harm quickly.

section6.title: 6. Legal compliance & authority reporting
section6.content: We comply with applicable child safety laws in the regions where we operate. Where appropriate, we report apparent CSAM to relevant organizations and authorities (for example, national hotlines or cybercrime units) in accordance with legal obligations and industry practice.
section6.content2: We do not use CSAE content for any purpose other than removal, mandatory reporting, and lawful cooperation with investigations.

section7.title: 7. Parental & guardian contact
section7.content: If you are a parent, guardian, or educator and believe a minor is using ToldYa! unsafely, contact us immediately using the email below with the username and a description of your concern. We will investigate and take appropriate action.

sectionContact.title: 8. Designated child safety contact
sectionContact.content: The designated point of contact for child safety, CSAM prevention practices, and compliance with Google Play child safety standards is ready to respond to inquiries from platforms, regulators, and users:
sectionContact.content2: For general app support (non–child-safety), you may also contact zuki.apps.dev@gmail.com. For privacy requests, see our Privacy Policy.

childSafety.metaDescription: ToldYa! published child safety standards against CSAE — zero tolerance, in-app reporting, moderation, legal compliance, and designated contact.

pilot.title: Now in pilot
pilot.message: ToldYa! is live on the App Store and Google Play as an invite-only pilot. Download the app, sign up with your invitation code, and start putting your predictions on the record.
pilot.inviteNote: Invite-only — you need a valid invitation code to register.
highlights: Post predictions your followers can see; vote and comment in the Arena
Category accuracy scores and a track record others can watch
English and Hebrew with RTL · dark and light themes
Free: no ads, no in-app purchases — reputation and bragging rights only

support.title: Support & Help
support.subtitle: We're here to help with ToldYa! — predictions, your account, privacy, and child safety.
support.metaDescription: Get help with ToldYa! — invite-only predictions, account deletion, privacy, child safety contact, and technical support.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.description: Have a question or need assistance? Send us an email and we'll get back to you as soon as possible.

q1.question: What is ToldYa!?
q1.answer: ToldYa! is an invite-only social app for predictions — post your call before it happens, vote in the Arena, build a reputation score, and prove you called it. Free on iOS and Android with no ads and no in-app purchases.

q2.question: Is ToldYa! invite-only?
q2.answer: Yes. During the pilot, access is invite-only. You need an invitation to create an account and use the social features.

q3.question: How do I delete my account?
q3.answer: Open the Delete Account page on zukiapps.com/toldya/delete-account for step-by-step instructions, or use in-app account settings where available. Deletion is permanent.

q4.question: Does ToldYa! have ads or in-app purchases?
q4.answer: No. ToldYa! is free with no ads and no in-app purchases in the current pilot release.

q5.question: Which languages are supported?
q5.answer: ToldYa! supports English and Hebrew, including full RTL layout for Hebrew.

q6.question: How do I report child safety concerns?
q6.answer: See our Child Safety Standards page at https://zukiapps.com/toldya/child-safety for CSAE policy and the designated child safety contact. For general support, email zuki.apps.dev@gmail.com.

q7.question: How does Arena voting work?
q7.answer: Post a prediction, then the community votes in the Arena on whether your call will happen. Results update your reputation score by category so you can track who called it right.

q9.question: How do prediction resolution and disputes work?
q9.answer: After the voting deadline, the prediction author must submit the outcome. Other members can dispute an outcome they believe is wrong through the in-app flow. Disputes are reviewed by our team and do not instantly change the result. See our Terms of Use for details.

q10.question: What are private rooms?
q10.answer: Private rooms let invited friends make predictions visible only within that room—not in public feeds or global leaderboards. The same community rules, reporting, and blocking apply inside rooms.

additionalHelp.title: Need More Help?
additionalHelp.description: If you couldn't find the answer you're looking for, please contact us. We typically respond within 24–48 hours.
